stories.title: AI did not just demand more electricity — it demanded water. By 2025, U.S. data centres, increasingly expanded for AI workloads, were consuming nearly one trillion litres of water a year, much of it used in cooling systems that shed heat by evaporating water i
stories.simple_summary: AI data centers (the huge computer buildings that power AI) are using nearly a trillion liters of water every year to cool down all their equipment, because running AI creates a lot of heat.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters to everyone because it uses precious water resources in places that might already have water shortages, similar to how a factory using too much of a shared town well affects the whole community.

stories.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
stories.simple_summary: TechCrunch made a guide explaining all the confusing AI words people use.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a helpful reference if you get lost reading about AI, but it's not news about anything that actually changed.

stories.title: Libertyville middle school teacher charged after authorities say he created AI child sex abuse materials
stories.simple_summary: A teacher was arrested for using AI to create illegal child abuse images.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a serious crime showing that AI tools can be misused for terrible purposes, and it's a reminder that these technologies need rules and enforcement.

stories.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock: Alphabet vs. Micron Technology (the Winner May Surprise You)
stories.simple_summary: A financial writer is comparing two tech companies' stock prices to say which AI investment might be better.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors buying stocks — nothing you need to act on unless you're managing retirement investments.

stories.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
stories.simple_summary: Different web browsers (like Chrome and Safari) are competing with each other, and there are other options available to choose from.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're frustrated with your current browser, you could try a different one, but this is mainly just competition between tech companies.

stories.title: We can debate the ethics of AI but can’t seem to change course | Letters
stories.simple_summary: The author says people debate whether AI is ethical but society isn't actually changing how AI is being used.
stories.why_it_matters: This reflects a real problem: we talk about AI concerns but don't have strong enough rules or action plans yet.

stories.title: Gen Z isn’t happy about AI. Even when mom helped invent it.
stories.simple_summary: Young people don't like AI, even when their parents work in the AI industry.
stories.why_it_matters: This honestly doesn't affect you much unless you're a parent worried about what your kids think, but it shows generational disagreement about AI.

stories.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
stories.simple_summary: A company called Anthropic is building AI tools to help scientists discover new medicines by organizing information and making pictures of data.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually lead to new drugs and treatments faster, but it's very early-stage and mostly matters to pharmaceutical researchers right now.

stories.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
stories.simple_summary: A company called Midjourney (known for making AI pictures) showed a video of a new ultrasound machine they're developing, but hasn't yet proven it actually works well.
stories.why_it_matters: If this ever works, it could provide cheap medical scans without radiation, but for now it's just an idea — don't expect changes to your doctor visits soon.

stories.title: Argentina's plan for AI-run companies can't avoid humans
stories.simple_summary: Argentina is planning to allow companies run entirely by AI, but they're realizing that's not completely possible — humans are still needed somewhere.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to investors and policy experts debating the future; it won't affect your daily life right now.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
stories.simple_summary: Mark Zuckerberg (who runs Meta) told his employees that their AI isn't improving as quickly as he expected it to.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech investors and Meta employees — it shows AI progress isn't as fast as some hype suggests, but nothing changes for regular people.

stories.title: Artificial intelligence: Yann LeCun works on more flexible AI
stories.simple_summary: A famous AI scientist is working on making AI that's more flexible and adaptable rather than just good at one specific task.
stories.why_it_matters: This is early research that might make AI better in the future, but it's mainly for engineers and scientists to follow.
